notes: Triggering is the dominant failure mode. should_trigger MUST fire; should_not_trigger are near-misses that must NOT pull this in (browser/React, the textmode.js library, or text-banner tasks); evals run scripts/image_to_ascii.py and verify the output shape.
should_trigger: Make some ASCII art of a cat.
Convert this image to ASCII art in Python with Pillow.
Turn this logo PNG into an ASCII text file I can paste in a README.
Batch-convert a folder of photos to ASCII .txt files.
Render this photo as ASCII and save it as a PNG with green text on black.
I want sharp-edged ASCII from this image, not the usual blurry brightness-ramp output.
Write me a command-line tool that turns a screenshot into ASCII art.
should_not_trigger: Add an image-to-ASCII effect to my React site.
Build a real-time textmode.js sketch that turns my webcam into ASCII.
Generate an ASCII banner of the word HELLO with figlet.
Print colored text in my terminal with ANSI escape codes.
Theme my <AsciiImage> component green-on-black with CSS variables.

evals.prompt: Convert this image to ASCII at 80 columns and print it.
evals.expected_output: Runs scripts/image_to_ascii.py with --cols 80; output is 80-char-wide monospace ASCII whose glyphs follow the image's contours (shape-aware: diagonals/curves traced with /, \, _, etc.), not a uniform fill or a single brightness ramp.

evals.prompt: Render this image as a PNG with white text on black and save it.
evals.expected_output: Produces a .png drawn with the bundled monospace font, white foreground on black background (--format png --fg white --bg black, with --invert for correct light-on-dark tones), matching the ASCII text output.

evals.prompt: Crank the contrast so the edges in this image are sharper.
evals.expected_output: Raises --contrast (e.g. to 4) and/or enables --directional; boundaries between light and dark regions get crisper without destroying smooth gradients.

evals.prompt: Turn this logo into an ASCII text file for my README.
evals.expected_output: Runs the converter with --out logo.txt (default dark-on-light polarity, no ANSI escapes in the file), a sensible --cols for a README, producing a clean monospace .txt.
